attacking_style: Pure sprinter who launches in final 200-400m, often from sub-optimal positions due to weak leadout. Shows tactical intelligence to surf wheels and read gaps, but frequently initiates too early (250-450m) burning matches before the line.
team_tactics: Chronically under-supported: BORA/UAE leadouts consistently fade early or position him poorly. Single domestique (GIBBONS) sometimes more effective than full trains. Wins often come from solo positioning intelligence rather than organized delivery.
terrain_preference: Flat, technical finishes with late directional changes suit his gap-reading ability. Struggles on: gradient finishes (loses momentum going wide), high-speed flat drags (pure power deficit), and rolling/punchy circuits where dropped early.
vulnerability: Premature sprint initiation in headwinds or from distance. Positioning errors in chaotic finishes (crashes, boxed in barriers). Fades when leadout delivers perfectly but lacks final 200m snap against top-tier sprinters. Poor form management when doing work earlier in stage.
key_patterns: Wins despite minimal team support (5+ observations), revealing elite solo positioning over leadout dependency
Launches 250-450m out repeatedly, arriving empty at line in headwinds or gradients (6+ times)
Crashes/positioning errors in technical finales touching wheels near barriers (3 observations)
Second/third places when leadout works perfectly, suggesting power deficit vs elite sprinters (8+ times)
Tactical intelligence: deliberately brakes to use rivals as leadout, reads hesitation gaps instantly (multiple)
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 Giro win after 4-year drought shows resurgence, but crash chasing ceremonial win and earlier form management issues suggest inconsistency persists despite improved positioning skills
